title: Module 1 quiz: How the auction works

questions.prompt: What does the auction actually rank ads on?

choices.text: Total value: bid times estimated action rate times ad quality. A great ad with strong predicted relevance can outbid a higher dollar bid from a weaker ad. The system optimizes for predicted user value, not pure dollar bids.

questions.explanation: Total value, not bid alone. Predicted relevance is the lever.

questions.prompt: Optimizing for link clicks but not getting purchases. Fix?

choices.text: Optimize for purchases instead. EAR predicts the event you specified. Click optimization finds clickers; purchase optimization finds buyers — different ML signals. Switch the optimization event to the conversion you want, with pixel + CAPI sending purchase events accurately.

questions.explanation: Optimize for the event you actually want.

questions.prompt: Default bid strategy for new campaigns?

choices.text: Lowest cost with daily budget. System bids dynamically to maximize results. Caps need historical CPA data to set sensibly; on new campaigns they typically underspend. Add caps later if predictable cost matters more than volume.

questions.explanation: Lowest cost for new campaigns; add caps after data.

questions.prompt: Why does fragmenting budget across many ad sets hurt performance?

choices.text: ML needs roughly 50 conversion events per ad set per week to exit learning phase. Many ad sets at low spend means none reaches the threshold; all remain exploratory with high CPA variance. Consolidate to fewer ad sets that each get learning-phase volume.

questions.explanation: Consolidate budget; let ad sets reach learning threshold.

questions.prompt: Three Ad Relevance Diagnostics rankings tell you what?

choices.text: Quality, engagement rate, and conversion rate rankings vs competing ads in the same audience — Below Average / Average / Above Average. Below Average dimensions reduce effective bid; the diagnostic tells you which dimension to fix. Quality = creative + post-click; engagement = hook + format; conversion = landing page + pixel accuracy.

questions.explanation: Three rankings = which dimension to fix.
